Feedback on CBT-1 Aion2: (typically for my favorite class: Elementalist/Spirit Master)
1. Graphics are awesome
2. Gameplay are cool
3. Summons Spirit and the Summoners skills are cool

But,
4. The summoners/main character can't perform any attacks or skills freely until there's an enemy or enemies nearby/targeted
5. The Spirit summons can't attack automatically (not really smart) until the summoner/main character perfoms an attack
6. The Spirit summons are weak, perhaps there're some upgradeable leveling-up

The rest is ok for now. Maybe the other classes combat system also the same as mine. If these suggestions above can be revamped before released then the gameplay will be more attractives and addictives in the future to explore the open-world mmorpg genre. Keep-up the good works. Pay-to-Win kills longevity
Extra dungeon entries, gear boosts, and stat advantages drive players away. PvP-PvE dies if wallet size decides the winner.

Cosmetic-only monetization works
Skins, wings and mounts items are highly desired without breaking balance. Players spend more when it doesn’t hurt fairness.

Aion 1 had the best cosmetic system
Collecting flowers for dyes, re-skinning any armor, and true fashion freedom gave the game identity. If Aion 2 removes or paywalls this, it feels like a downgrade.

Community identity matters
Fashion, housing, and open-world gathering gave players reasons to stay beyond stats. P2W undermines that sense of individuality.

Trust & reputation
NCSoft already struggles with player trust. If Aion 2 repeats the same mistakes, veterans—the core audience—will leave and spread negativity.

PC vs Mobile controls
Aion 2 should be designed around PC-style controls (mouse + keyboard, full skill rotation, camera control), not simplified mobile-style inputs. The depth and precision of gameplay is a core part of the series.


👉 In short: P2W = short-lived hype, fast death. Freedom = loyal community, long life.

Translation:


Hello, I’m Kim Nam-jun, PD for AION 2.
Since our first broadcast, so many of you have shared a wide variety of opinions and comments. Thanks to that, our development team feels an even greater sense of responsibility, and we’re fully focused on development right now.

Today, I wanted to take some time to read through those valuable comments you left after the first broadcast and respond to your voices together. (Wow, that came out a bit awkwardly, didn’t it?)

On the graphics
I’ve seen comments like, “Wow, those graphics are insane. I’m definitely going to have to try this.”
Reading those really makes me happy. I think our art team will be especially thrilled — they’ve been working extremely hard to make everything look beautiful.

We’ve had requests for both Eastern-style and Western-style characters, and even something right in between. Even though it was a tall order, the team worked hard and I think we’ve struck a good balance. Among current MMORPGs, we’re pushing hard to deliver the best graphics possible.

Someone said, “Well, at least the character’s pretty.”
Oh, so just the character? (laughs) Our backgrounds are beautiful too! Not just the characters — the whole world is being crafted with a lot of care. When you actually play, you’ll feel something very different and much better than what you get from just looking at screenshots.

On character designs
I’ve seen comments like, “Female character designs look pretty good.”
During our Focus Group Test, many players also said the male characters looked great. We’ve got a wide variety — some idol-like types, some rugged “man’s man” types. We didn’t know exactly what you’d want, so… we prepared everything.

On skies and environments
There was also a comment about how the surrounding sky and distant scenery were amazing. Since flying is such a big part of the game, we put a lot of effort into the skies and vistas. I’m glad that’s coming through, and I hope you’ll come see it for yourself.

On the gameplay trailer
I’ve seen concerns about the gameplay trailer. Honestly, we didn’t have time to produce a cinematic — we just captured footage directly from the game. Combat clips came from the combat team, background shots from the background team, and so on. What you saw is the real in-game graphics.

I personally think the game looks even better when you’re actually playing. I log in once or twice a day just to mess with the character customization, and every time I’m struck by how good it looks in-game — especially with lighting.

Next time, I plan to show character customization live, so you can see for yourself.

On the UI
Some people have said, “In the next YouTube Live, it would be nice to see actual development screens, including the in-game UI. We can’t trust it since so many mobile games hide this stuff.”
Alright — next live broadcast, we’ll show actual gameplay with the real UI on screen. Consider this the first reveal.

On unrevealed content
We still have so much content we haven’t shown yet. Before the official launch, we’ll keep revealing more through live streams, so I hope you’ll join us for those.

On nostalgic AION memories
One comment said, “I miss the old Rift battles. Traveling through space-time to mess around was so much fun.”
We know that excitement, and we’re making sure you can experience that again in AION 2.

Another person recalled, “The real legend was Dredgion. Over 100 people waiting at the Rift entrance, but only 30 could get in.”
Those kinds of memories and emotions mean a lot to me too, and I want to bring that feeling back. We’re doing our very best.

Reading all these comments directly in front of the camera feels different — it really hits home. I want to let you know we’re sincerely listening to your voices and preparing accordingly.

You’ll be able to see the true face of AION 2 through our live streams before launch, so please look forward to it.
And don’t forget to subscribe to the AION 2 channel and turn on notifications.

Thank you!
